title: Sync does not seem to make use of docker.io credentials
body:
Consider the following YAML file:

```yaml
docker.io:
  images:
    amazon/aws-node-termination-handler: []
    directxman12/k8s-prometheus-adapter-amd64: []
    hashicorp/consul-k8s: []
    kubernetesui/metrics-scraper: []
    nvidia/k8s-device-plugin: []
    victoriametrics/vmagent: []
    victoriametrics/vmalert: []
    victoriametrics/vminsert: []
    victoriametrics/vmselect: []
    victoriametrics/vmstorage: []
```

When invoked with `skopeo sync --src yaml --dest docker quay.yaml quay.io/acme`, I get rate limit requests error.

```
FATA[0069] Error copying tag "docker://victoriametrics/vminsert:heads-cluster-0-gb5f491a3": Error initializing source docker://victoriametrics/vminsert:heads-cluster-0-gb5f491a3: Error reading manifest heads-cluster-0-gb5f491a3 in docker.io/victoriametrics/vminsert: toomanyrequests: You have reached your pull rate limit. You may increase the limit by authenticating and upgrading: https://www.docker.com/increase-rate-limit
```

**I am logged into a paid Dockerhub account.** When I manually pull the affected image, it goes fine. 

```
$ docker pull victoriametrics/vminsert:heads-cluster-0-gb5f491a3
heads-cluster-0-gb5f491a3: Pulling from victoriametrics/vminsert
7ea761c3bb7f: Pull complete 
d1154548775f: Pull complete 
Digest: sha256:edfb46ac9f0b3649b4b7e8ac75f7f23d5ee49563c8f9056dbfe323cdbf5db4b8
Status: Downloaded newer image for victoriametrics/vminsert:heads-cluster-0-gb5f491a3
docker.io/victoriametrics/vminsert:heads-cluster-0-gb5f491a3
```

When I manually add the credentials into the YAML file, the sync works and I don't hit the rate limit.

Contents of `~/.docker/config.json` redacted:

```json
{
        "auths": {
                "https://index.docker.io/v1/": {
                        "auth": "<snip>"
                },
                "quay.io": {
                        "auth": "<snip>"
                }
        },
        "HttpHeaders": {
                "User-Agent": "Docker-Client/19.03.14 (linux)"
        },
        "credHelpers": {
                "asia.gcr.io": "gcloud",
                "eu.gcr.io": "gcloud",
                "gcr.io": "gcloud",
                "marketplace.gcr.io": "gcloud",
                "staging-k8s.gcr.io": "gcloud",
                "us.gcr.io": "gcloud"
        }
}
```

Other commands don't seem to face this issue:

```
$ skopeo copy docker://victoriametrics/vminsert:heads-cluster-0-gb5f491a3 dir://$(pwd)/temp
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 7ea761c3bb7f done  
Copying blob d1154548775f done  
Copying config 033c10d7b7 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```

comments.body: The error suggests authenticating and upgrading the pull rate limit, it does not seem like an issue with the dockerhub login credential.

Thanks for your report.
> ```
> DEBU[0017] Returning credentials from DockerAuthConfig
> ```

This looks like a real bug to me, https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/1b813f805bccb7da280fb611da051a6a0e43683e/cmd/skopeo/sync.go#L283 is always non-`nil`, which turns off reading the config files.
If you just remove that line, does it fix the problem?
Just removing that line “fixes” the problem by breaking the command for users that actually supply credentials in that field. It’s not a very difficult thing to fix properly, sure.

I'm hitting this issue as well. I verified `skopeo` is not using my `docker.io` credential by purposefully corrupting the token in `auth.json` and `skopeo` continues syncing instead of failing to authenticate.

I got around this by putting the credential for `docker.io` repositories in the YAML file. Not ideal, but it does work.
Thanks for the fix! May I know when this might be cut into a release?

With this and a couple of other fixes that we've had recently, we probably ought to spin up a new release in the not too distant future.  Perhaps in time for the next RHEL train on the 18th?  @rhatdan @mtrmac WDYT?
The vendor dance ~implies having to make a release — if one is planned.
I am getting ready to do one today.
